items.title: China aims to ‘infiltrate’ US-Mexico-Canada trade deal, says American manufacturing group
items.creator: Mia Nurmamat,Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China aims to ‘infiltrate’ US-Mexico-Canada trade deal, says American manufacturing group
items.content:
China is trying to “infiltrate” the US-Mexico-Canada trade agreement via Mexican automotive investments, a US manufacturing trade group said, as Washington indicated it would not renew the agreement that comes up for review this month.
However, analysts cautioned that full decoupling from China’s automotive industry remains difficult, given North America’s continued dependence on Chinese-sourced motor vehicle components and Mexico’s legacy of export manufacturing plus quick access to the US...

items.title: Why the World Bank is winding down loans to China amid a ‘natural’ shift
items.creator: Sylvia Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why the World Bank is winding down loans to China amid a ‘natural’ shift
items.content:
Beijing has downplayed reports that the World Bank will phase out lending to China by 2031, with the Ministry of Finance noting that the nation’s advancing economy and changing development needs were shifting cooperation away from financing and towards knowledge sharing.
“This is the natural result of changing domestic needs and the transformation of bilateral cooperation,” the ministry said in a statement on Wednesday, referring to the World Bank’s declining lending to China in recent...

items.title: European companies ‘becoming more reliant on China, not less’, EU chamber chief says
items.creator: Huizhao Huang
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: European companies ‘becoming more reliant on China, not less’, EU chamber chief says
items.content:
Europe’s push to reduce its dependence on China is driving many of its companies deeper into Chinese supply chains, according to the head of the European Union Chamber of Commerce in China.
“Europe is becoming more dependent on China, not less,” chamber president Jens Eskelund said, adding that for European firms, staying competitive increasingly meant embedding themselves more deeply in the supply chains Brussels wanted them to move away from.
He said Europe had fundamentally misread what China...

items.title: Lorenzi warns of economic conflict and unplottable solutions: ‘world without bearings’
items.creator: Xiaofei Xu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Lorenzi warns of economic conflict and unplottable solutions: ‘world without bearings’

images.alt: Jean-Herve Lorenzi, president of Le Cercle des economistes, contends that major European powers must reach a strategic agreement with China. Photo: Les Rencontres Eco
images.title: Jean-Herve Lorenzi, president of Le Cercle des economistes, contends that major European powers must reach a strategic agreement with China. Photo: Les Rencontres Eco

items.content:
The global order has lost the structural anchors that once ensured stability, entering a volatile period of economic conflict with “no obvious solutions”, the head of a prominent French economic think tank has cautioned.
Reindustrialising “is far more difficult than it was 30 years ago”, said Jean-Herve Lorenzi, president of Le Cercle des economistes, which organises France’s flagship annual economic forum, Les Rencontres Economiques d’Aix-en-Provence. “If we had each defended our own countries’...

items.title: In the AI era, US-China competition hinges on who can adapt faster
items.creator: Marco Vicenzino
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: In the AI era, US-China competition hinges on who can adapt faster
items.content:
Power has historically been measured by indicators such as territory, population, industrial production, military capability and economic scale. Those foundations remain indispensable. But each technological revolution has changed the nature of geopolitical competition.
The Industrial Revolution rewarded manufacturing capacity. The 20th century rewarded industrial scale, military mobilisation and ideological reach. Globalisation rewarded efficiency, integration and financial connectivity. The...

items.translated_title: Visa-free ‘dividends’: China drawing a boom in foreign visitors this summer
items.content:
Nikolas Karageorge has mapped out a tour of China that starts in Shanghai and Beijing before heading deep inland – venturing well beyond the typical tourist trail.
“ It’s an ambitious itinerary, I know!” said the 37-year-old resident of Melbourne, Australia, about his path through Chengdu, Chongqing, Zhangjiajie, Guizhou, Guilin and finally Guangzhou. “I’ve heard China is very safe and easy to travel around so this is a great opportunity, as Australians currently have 30 days of visa-free...

items.title: China’s new investment law asserts control over offshore tech transfers in landmark move
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s new investment law asserts control over offshore tech transfers in landmark move
items.content:
China has enacted a sweeping overseas-investment law that aims to safeguard national interests against trade barriers and unauthorised use of advanced technology abroad, though analysts warn that the move could complicate operations for foreign partners.
The State Council’s 34-article Regulation on Overseas Investment, taking effect on Wednesday, authorises “necessary and defensive measures” to protect Chinese investors and interests overseas in response to foreign trade-related barriers.
Under...

items.title: China’s grip on global ship repair worries Japan. Can Tokyo end its dependence?
items.creator: Ralph Jennings
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China’s grip on global ship repair worries Japan. Can Tokyo end its dependence?
items.content:
A 370 trillion yen (US$2.28 trillion) industrial upgrade plan unveiled by Japanese officials could help revive the country’s ship repair sector as Tokyo seeks to reduce reliance on China, which now dominates the global market, analysts said.
The plan, approved by Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi’s cabinet, targets 17 strategic industries, including semiconductors, AI and shipping, including the repair of large seagoing vessels.
About 100 billion yen (US$616 million) would be allocated to...

items.title: Why the AI future won’t be decided by algorithms and chatbots
items.creator: Syed Munir Khasru
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Why the AI future won’t be decided by algorithms and chatbots
items.content:
When people talk about the race for artificial intelligence, they usually focus on software. Headlines revolve around ChatGPT, Gemini, DeepSeek or the latest breakthrough model.
Governments announce AI strategies and investors pour billions into start-ups promising to transform everything from medicine to education. Nonetheless, the most consequential battle in the AI age may not be over algorithms at all. It may be over the machines.
Behind every chatbot response and AI-generated image lies a...

items.title: China-Canada trade thaw falters as Beijing levies penalties for dumping pea starch
items.creator: Emma Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China-Canada trade thaw falters as Beijing levies penalties for dumping pea starch

images.alt: White pea starch powder is seen on a wooden spoon in a cast-iron pan. Photo: Shutterstock
images.title: White pea starch powder is seen on a wooden spoon in a cast-iron pan. Photo: Shutterstock

items.content:
Beijing has issued a preliminary verdict finding that pea-starch products imported from Canada were dumped in China, following Ottawa’s anti-dumping investigation into Chinese-made steel racks.
“Relevant authorities have made a preliminary determination that imported pea-starch products originating in Canada have been dumped,” the Ministry of Commerce said in a statement on Tuesday. “The dumping acts have caused material damage to the domestic pea-starch industry.”
Starting on Wednesday,...

items.title: China-EU joint statement on trade
items.creator: SCMP Plus
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China-EU joint statement on trade
items.content:
China and the European Union released a joint statement on Monday following talks between China’s Minister of Commerce, Wang Wentao, and the EU’s trade chief, Maros Sefcovic, in Brussels. During the meeting, the two sides agreed to establish a joint platform to monitor trade flows and to set up working groups to ease bilateral economic tensions.

EU-China Joint Statement by scmp

items.title: China’s EV boom driven by local governments, not Beijing’s industrial policy, study finds

items.translated_title: China’s EV boom driven by local governments, not Beijing’s industrial policy, study finds
items.content:
Adaptability and partnerships between local authorities and private capital have fuelled the rapid ascent of China’s electric vehicle (EV) industry, not simply Beijing’s headline-grabbing industrial policy, according to a new study.
As private EV makers in the world’s second-largest economy overtake state-owned carmakers and make formidable inroads into overseas markets, including Europe, the research found that Beijing’s industrial policy and subsidies played only a limited role in their...

items.title: China opens new Central Asia rail link to strengthen overland trade amid shipping risks

items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China opens new Central Asia rail link to strengthen overland trade amid shipping risks
items.content:
A new 5,420 km (3,367 miles) rail service connecting western China with Turkmenistan has launched, expanding the country’s trade network in Central Asia as part of Beijing’s intensifying efforts to diversify overland routes and bolster supply chain resilience.
The inaugural train departed on Sunday from Xining, capital of Qinghai province, loaded with electrical appliances, auto parts, clothing and other daily necessities, state broadcaster CCTV reported. The 55-container goods train is expected...

items.title: Concern over AI’s impact on Asia’s real estate sector is misplaced

items.translated_title: Concern over AI’s impact on Asia’s real estate sector is misplaced
items.content:
Artificial intelligence (AI) is a transformative force in economies and financial markets. McKinsey estimates that data centres alone will need a staggering US$6.7 trillion in capital investment across the global data centre value chain in the next five years, and the scale of the investment suggests the world economy is in the early stages of a far-reaching shift that could lead to big gains in productivity.
In a report on June 24, Vanguard said the massive buildout of AI infrastructure...

items.title: China adds 20 Japanese entities to export control list over remilitarisation concerns (原标题: China adds 20 Japanese entities to export-control list over remilitarisation concerns)
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: China adds 20 Japanese entities to export control list over remilitarisation concerns
items.content:
The Ministry of Commerce said on Monday that to deter Japan’s “remilitarisation” and its attempts to “acquire nuclear weapons”, it has added 20 Japanese entities “involved in enhancing Japan’s military capabilities” to an export-control list.
Twenty Japanese entities have also been added to a separate watch list.
The moves were made to safeguard China’s national security and interests and to fulfil international obligations such as non-proliferation, the ministry added.
A ministry spokesperson...

items.title: Economist Richard Koo on China and Japan’s shared ‘battle against time’
items.creator: Frank Chen
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Economist Richard Koo on China and Japan’s shared ‘battle against time’
items.content:
Nomura Research Institute chief economist Richard Koo is well-known for his “balance sheet recession” interpretation of Japan’s economic downfall in the 1990s. In his second Open Questions interview, Koo assesses the risks the Chinese economy is facing this year and beyond, the many parallels between Beijing and Tokyo, and why market size and technology alone may not be enough to help avert a fate similar to Japan’s.
Koo also explains why giving China’s consumers money will not help and why...

items.title: How the AI boom exposes investors to risk, while a downturn could see a sharp crash: BIS
items.creator: Daisy Wu
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: How the AI boom exposes investors to risk, while a downturn could see a sharp crash: BIS
items.content:
As the global financial system struggles to keep pace with the artificial intelligence investment boom and capital flows surge through loosely regulated, non-bank channels, a key international organisation has warned that an AI downturn could develop into a sharper, faster crash than a traditional banking crisis.
In its annual economic report released on Sunday, the Bank for International Settlements (BIS) said funding for AI was increasingly channelled through hedge funds, private credit...

items.title: For an Africa seeking growth, China is proving a reliable partner
items.creator: Göktuğ Çalışkan
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: For an Africa seeking growth, China is proving a reliable partner

images.alt: A truck with the first batch of fresh avocados grown in Kenya and destined for the Chinese market departs from a factory in Limuru, Kiambu County, Kenya, on August 2, 2022. Photo: Xinhua
images.title: A truck with the first batch of fresh avocados grown in Kenya and destined for the Chinese market departs from a factory in Limuru, Kiambu County, Kenya, on August 2, 2022. Photo: Xinhua

items.content:
On June 4, at a packing facility in Limuru near Nairobi, Kenya, avocados were being prepared for the Chinese market. The scene may seem mundane, but the politics behind it is significant.
China has removed tariffs on imports from 53 African countries. African exporters are beginning to think about Beijing less as a distant buyer than as a market that could reshape their margins.
When the UN projected 4 per cent gross domestic product growth for Africa for 2026, the figure landed quietly. No...

items.title: China’s big trucks go electric and abroad as subsidies help pave road to net-zero freight
items.creator: Daniel Ren

items.translated_title: China’s big trucks go electric and abroad as subsidies help pave road to net-zero freight
items.content:
Domestic makers of heavy-duty trucks are emerging as the latest beneficiaries of accelerating electrification on China’s roads, as technological gains and lower ownership costs are bolstering overseas sales amid a global energy crisis.
Southeast Asia and Africa, where Chinese makers have already established overseas assembly hubs, were expected to serve as new growth engines for companies ranging from FAW Jiefang to Foton Commercial Vehicles, according to analysts at S&P Global Ratings.
The two...

items.title: Beijing is building Shanghai into a major offshore yuan centre. Should Hong Kong worry?
items.creator: Sylvia Ma,Emma Ma
items.summary: 无法生成摘要（API请求失败）。
items.translated_title: Beijing is building Shanghai into a major offshore yuan centre. Should Hong Kong worry?
items.content:
For the better part of two decades, Hong Kong has served as the undisputed poster child for Beijing’s ambitions to internationalise the yuan.
But in a world increasingly defined by sanctions, frozen assets and fears of US dollar weaponisation, policymakers are beginning to confront a strategic question: can a single offshore centre shoulder the burden of the yuan’s global ambitions?
That question lies at the heart of Beijing’s latest efforts to expand Shanghai’s offshore finance role, reviving a...
